history.author: Albert Thomas
history.titleZh: Arctic Freezer 36-S 评测：小尺寸，高效能，低价格
history.descriptionZh:
Arctic 此前凭借 Liquid Freezer III Pro 360mm 一体式水冷等产品，在高端散热领域树立了“大尺寸、强性能”的形象。但并非所有用户都需要厚实的 360mm 水冷排，许多小机箱甚至无法安装水冷；同时，对大多数普通消费者而言，基础风冷已足够应对日常负载。为此，Arctic 推出了 Freezer 36-S——一款售价不到 30 美元的入门级塔式风冷散热器，采用单风扇、单塔结构，通过四根直触式热管连接。

**核心技术与设计亮点：**
- **四根直触式铜热管**：热管略微错位排列，以优化热分布，将 CPU 热量高效传导至鳍片。散热塔的背面为平整设计，正面（朝向风扇）则采用“链锯”状纹路，增加扰流效果。
- **Intel 防弯扣具**：针对 Intel LGA1700/1851 平台附带防弯 CPU 接触框架，可保证长期使用中接触面平整，尤其适合高频重负载场景（减少热循环导致的形变）。但安装不当可能引发内存不稳等问题，不建议初学者使用。
- **P12 Pro 高速风扇**：Arctic 自家的 P12 Pro，最高转速 3000 RPM，提供 77 CFM 风量和 6.9 mmH₂O 静压——纸面参数是市面多数风扇的 2~3 倍，兼顾高风量与强穿透力。
- **MX-7 导热硅脂**：随附 0.8g 管装 MX-7（最新款），其前代 MX-6 在 Tom's Hardware 测试中性能表现出色，与市面顶级硅脂相当。
- **环保包装**：仅使用纸板材料，无塑料袋。

**规格与性能参数：**
- 颜色：银/黑、白
- 售价：基础版 28.49 美元，白色 ARGB 版 34.99 美元
- 保修：6 年
- 兼容性：AMD AM5、Intel LGA1700/1851
- 散热塔尺寸：128×88×120 mm（长×宽×高）
- 实测最大 TDP：>245 W（搭配 AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D）

**安装说明：**
AMD 平台需移除原装扣具，换上附带的安装支架，涂硅脂，用螺丝固定散热塔，最后通过风扇夹安装 P12 Pro 风扇并连接主板。Intel 平台须先安装防弯接触框架，其余步骤类似。整体过程相对简单，但 Intel 框架需注意安装精度。

**测试方法与真实性能：**
为贴近实际使用场景，Tom's Hardware 采用封闭机箱（Tryx FLOVA F50，并加装一枚 Noctua NF-A12 G2 进风风扇）进行测试，避免开放式平台带来虚高的性能假象。测试 CPU 为 Ryzen 9 9950X3D，其 CCD 热分布比非 3D 版 9950X 更均衡（后者双 CCD 温差可达 10°C 以上），因此结果更具代表性。在此配置下，Freezer 36-S 成功压制超过 245W 的 TDP，证明其作为百元级单塔风冷，已具备不俗的散热潜力。

**技术影响与应用场景：**
Freezer 36-S 延续了 Arctic 高性价比的路线，将原本属于中高端散热器的直触热管、高速静压风扇等技术下放到入门价位，使预算有限或机箱空间狭小的用户也能获得可靠的散热性能。它尤其适合以下场景：
- 预算敏感、追求高性价比的普通装机用户；
- 使用小机箱（如 ITX/mATX）且无法安装水冷或双塔风冷；
- 搭配中端或主流 CPU（如 Ryzen 5/7、Core i5/i7）日常使用，甚至轻度超频；
- 需要低噪音、良好风道穿透力的场景（P12 Pro 在低转速下仍能维持不错的风量与静压）。

总体而言，Freezer 36-S 证明了入门级风冷不必在性能上妥协太多，在 30 美元价位提供了扎实的散热能力，是低价位高性价比市场中的有力竞争者。

history.title: SK hynix unveils 'iHBM' thermal architecture that cools AI memory at the source — integrated cooling elements inside HBM interface cut thermal resistance by 30%, target next-gen HBM5 accelerators and dense AI data centers
history.description: SK hynix has unveiled iHBM, a new thermal packaging architecture that embeds cooling elements directly into the HBM interface layer, reducing thermal resistance by 30% and helping future AI accelerators avoid performance-killing thermal throttling.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter SK hynix announced iHBM today, a memory heat management technology designed to enhance AI system performance. The thermal packaging solution improves heat dissipation by integrating ICEs (integrated cooling elements) directly into the HBM package. SK hynix says the result is an over 30% reduction in thermal resistance, “ensuring stable operating characteristics even in high-temperature and high-load environments.”Go deeper with TH Premium: Memory (Image credit: SK Hynix)AI data centers are swallowing the world's memory and storage supplyThe future of DRAM: From DDR5 to future ICsHigh-bandwidth memory roadmapHere's why HBM is coming for your PC's RAMThe iHBM architecture embeds non-conductive silicon cooling elements directly into the Die-to-Die Physical Layer (D2D PHY), the critical, high-speed connection interface between the HBM base die and the AI processor, which is prone to high temperature spikes as a result of extreme data traffic. By placing cooling elements in this layer, SK hynix mitigates the severe thermal throttling that cripples AI system performance during heavy computational workloads.The company believes that structurally preventing thermal throttling will enable next-generation memory layers (targeted for future generations like HBM5) to scale to higher stack heights and sustain maximum data transfer speeds under the heavy computational loads of AI data centers.Latest Videos From You may like Intel, SK hynix shares surge following reports of chip packaging partnership — SK is said to be testing Intel's 2.5D EMIB for HBM integration SK hynix and SanDisk announce speedy HBF flash chip standard NEO Semiconductor's revolutionary 3D X-DRAM for AI processors has passed proof-of-concept validation “iHBM is the optimal solution for minimizing heat generation developed by combining memory design capabilities and advanced packaging technology,” said SK hynix Vice President Lee Kang-wook. “We will proactively provide the value customers need in the AI environment and further solidify our leadership in AI memory.”SK hynix plans to apply iHBM technology from next-generation products, such as HBM5, to meet the thermal management requirements of high-performance computing (HPC), AI data centers, and other ultra-high-density and ultra-high-bandwidth environments, thereby improving overall system stability and efficiency. A conceptual diagram of the ‘iHBM Solution’ unveiled by SK hynix (Image credit: SK Hynix)Heat management is one of the biggest challenges facing HBM (High-Bandwidth Memory) technology. Unlike conventional memory, HBM achieves massive bandwidth by vertically stacking multiple DRAM dies, dramatically shortening the distance data must travel and enabling far higher transfer speeds with better power efficiency.To minimize latency and feed AI processors fast enough to avoid bottlenecks, HBM is placed extremely close to the GPU or AI accelerator on the same package, connected through a high-speed silicon interposer. However, this dense arrangement also creates severe thermal problems.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsThe Die-to-Die Physical Layer (D2D PHY) — the ultra-high-speed interface linking the processor and HBM stacks — continuously moves terabytes of data per second. As thousands of signaling lanes and billions of transistors switch at extremely high frequencies, switching losses, leakage current, and electrical resistance generate substantial heat.The problem is compounded by the processor itself, which already produces enormous amounts of heat. With the HBM stacks packed tightly around the processor, heat accumulates rapidly in a very small area. When temperatures exceed safe limits, the system automatically reduces clock speeds and voltages through thermal throttling to prevent physical damage, lowering overall performance.SK hynix's new iHBM approach attempts to tackle the problem at the structural level. Unlike conventional HBM cooling designs that primarily dissipate heat indirectly through the core die and surrounding package structures, the company's iHBM architecture instead places Integrated Cooling Elements (ICEs) directly around the D2D PHY region — the exact zone where thermal concentration is most severe. This approach creates a dedicated dissipation path at the source, reducing overall thermal resistance by 30% and allowing the chip to maintain stable operation under the high-temperature, high-pressure conditions that dense AI workloads demand.SK hynix says the technology can be manufactured at scale using its existing Wafer Level Packaging (WLP) process, which is built on its Mass Reflow Molded Underfill (MR-MUF) packaging technology already used in commercial HBM products. The design is also architecturally compatible with existing System-in-Package configurations, meaning customers can integrate the new thermal capability without major redesigns. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
history.author: Etiido Uko
history.titleZh: SK海力士发布'iHBM'热架构，从源头冷却AI内存——HBM接口内集成冷却元件，热阻降低30%，瞄准下一代HBM5加速器和密集AI数据中心
history.descriptionZh:
SK海力士今日宣布推出iHBM内存散热管理技术，旨在通过创新封装方案解决AI系统中的热节流难题。该技术将集成冷却元件（ICEs）直接嵌入HBM封装内部，使热阻降低超过30%，确保芯片在高温高负载环境下仍能保持稳定运行特性。

**背景与上下文：** 高带宽内存（HBM）通过垂直堆叠多个DRAM晶粒实现超高带宽，并紧贴GPU或AI加速器放置，以缩短数据传输距离、降低延迟。然而，这种密集封装结构导致严重散热问题：Die-to-Die物理层（D2D PHY）作为连接HBM基础晶粒与AI处理器的高速接口，每秒需传输数TB数据，数千条信号通道和数十亿晶体管在极高频率下切换，产生的开关损耗、漏电流和电阻热效应使该区域成为热集中点。同时处理器自身也会产生巨大热量，在狭小空间内热量迅速累积。一旦温度超限，系统将自动触发热节流，降低时钟频率和电压，导致AI性能严重下降。

**核心技术原理与创新点：** iHBM架构在D2D PHY层直接嵌入非导电硅基冷却元件，精准放置于热集中最严重的区域。与传统间接通过核心晶粒和封装结构散热不同，这一方案在热源处建立专用散热路径，从结构层面遏制热节流。SK海力士表示，该设计可基于现有晶圆级封装（WLP）工艺制造，采用已在商用HBM产品中应用的Mass Reflow Molded Underfill（MR-MUF）技术，且与现有系统级封装（SiP）配置架构兼容，客户无需大幅重新设计即可集成新的散热能力。

**性能参数与对比：** 热阻降低超过30%是官方给出的核心参数，意味着在相同功耗下芯片温度显著下降，或允许在更高功率下运行而不触发节流。这为未来HBM5等更高堆叠层数的内存世代铺平道路，使其能在AI数据中心的重计算负载下维持最大数据传输速率。

**技术影响与应用场景：** iHBM将从下一代产品（如HBM5）开始应用，满足高性能计算（HPC）、AI数据中心及其他超高密度、超高带宽环境的散热需求，提升整体系统稳定性与效率。SK海力士副总裁Lee Kang-wook评价称：“iHBM是结合内存设计能力与先进封装技术的最优散热方案，我们将主动提供AI环境所需的价值，进一步巩固AI内存领导地位。”

这项技术的关键意义在于从结构上解决长期困扰HBM的散热瓶颈。随着AI模型规模持续膨胀，内存带宽需求呈指数级增长，传统散热方案已接近物理极限。iHBM通过在信号与散热之间建立协同设计，使存储堆叠能够在更高功耗密度下工作，为下一代AI加速器释放性能潜力提供了关键支撑。

history.title: Angry TSMC employees considering strikes, unionization over employee bonuses, report claims — company reportedly considering 15% payout cut to fund capex despite record revenues fuelled by AI surge
history.description: TSMC responded by saying it expects employee profit-sharing bonuses to grow at a faster rate in 2026 than they did in 2025.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 2 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter TSMC employees are openly discussing forming a union and staging a strike after rumors spread that the company plans to cut performance bonuses by approximately 15%, according to a DigiTimes report published today. The rumored haircut comes despite TSMC posting a record first-quarter net profit of NT$572.5 billion ($17.9 billion), a 58% year-over-year increase driven by surging AI chip demand. Workers say the company's historical practice of returning roughly 13% of retained earnings as employee bonuses has been cut, even as profits climb, and they’re pointing to Samsung’s recent union deal as a template for action.Go deeper with TH Premium: Chipmaking (Image credit: tsmc)A deeper look at the chipmaking supply chainTSMC's $165 billion U.S. investments examinedChina reportedly reverse-engineers EUV toolChina bets on DUV, as EUV blockade reshapes chipmakingTSMC responded by saying it expects employee profit-sharing bonuses to grow faster in 2026 than in 2025, and that it is "fully aware of its growing corporate social responsibility in Taiwan," Digitimes reported.The most likely explanation for the rumored cut, according to analysts cited by South Korean and Taiwanese media, is TSMC’s capex program. The company is spending $52 billion to $56 billion annually while constructing 12 new fabs across the U.S., Japan, Germany, and Taiwan to secure its lead in 2nm and 1.4nm manufacturing. That outlay appears to be tightening the cash available for employee compensation.Latest Videos From You may like Samsung chip workers reject $340,000 one-time bonus, demand annual payouts like SK hynix's $900,000 Samsung reportedly set to distribute up to $26.6 billion to staff in AI-driven semiconductor bonuses after last-minute union deal More than 30,000 Samsung union members take to the streets to demand higher cut of AI profits, an average of $400,000 per worker Based on 2025 earnings, the average TSMC employee bonus was approximately NT$2.64 million (roughly $87,000), with the total bonus pool reaching around NT$206.1 billion, according to Taiwan’s Liberty Times. Frustrations have risen around the ratio of profits going to employeesThe unrest at TSMC has intensified in the wake of Samsung’s landmark union deal last week. Samsung narrowly avoided an 18-day factory shutdown by agreeing to allocate 10.5% of its semiconductor division's operating profit as stock-based bonuses, plus another 1.5% in cash, over a 10-year period. That deal translates to projected average payouts of roughly $340,000 per chip division employee in 2026, based on recent estimates of Samsung's operating profit.SK hynix agreed to a similar structure last September, setting aside 10% of operating profit for employee bonuses. The comparison is undoubtedly painful for TSMC workers, who have no union and no formal mechanism to negotiate collectively; the company has operated without a labor union since its founding in 1987.Employee frustration has spilled onto Dcard, a Taiwanese workplace community, and dedicated TSMC Facebook pages, where workers have posted complaints ahead of TSMC's shareholder meeting scheduled for May 28th at the company's Hsinchu headquarters. Some have asked whether forming a union would violate Taiwanese law, and others argue that the company prioritizes shareholder returns and overseas expansion over its workforce.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsDoris Hsu, chairperson of silicon wafer manufacturer GlobalWafers, has weighed in on the broader debate, saying that across GlobalWafers' 18 factories in nine countries, some have unions and some don’t, and that the key factor in business performance is whether a company shares profits with workers, not whether a union exists.Samsung's deal, meanwhile, is already facing legal pushback. A shareholder lawsuit challenges the agreement on the grounds that committing a fixed percentage of operating profit to employee payouts over a decade conflicts with the capital demands of chipmaking at scale. history.titleZh: 据称台积电愤怒的员工考虑因员工奖金问题举行罢工、成立工会——尽管AI热潮推动创纪录营收，公司据报道考虑削减15%的奖金支付以资助资本支出
history.descriptionZh:
台积电（TSMC）员工正公开讨论组建工会并酝酿罢工，起因是公司内部传闻计划将绩效奖金削减约15%。这一消息由DigiTimes今日报道，尽管台积电刚刚交出一份创纪录的季度成绩单：2025年第一季度净利润达5725亿新台币（约179亿美元），同比增长58%，主要受AI芯片需求爆发的推动。员工指出，公司历来将约13%的留存收益作为员工分红，但如今在利润攀升之际，这一比例却被压缩。他们正将三星电子近期达成的工会协议视为行动的模板。

台积电回应称，预计2026年员工分红奖金增速将高于2025年，并且公司“充分意识到在台湾不断增长的企业社会责任”。但据韩国及台湾媒体引述分析师的观点，此次奖金削减传闻最可能的原因是台积电庞大的资本支出计划。该公司目前每年支出520亿至560亿美元，同时在美国、日本、德国和台湾建设12座新晶圆厂，以确保在2nm及1.4nm制程上的领先地位。这笔巨额开支似乎正在挤压可用于员工薪酬的现金。

根据台湾《自由时报》的数据，基于2025年盈利，台积电员工平均奖金约为264万新台币（约8.7万美元），总奖金池约达2061亿新台币。员工对利润分配比例的不满正在上升。

这场不满情绪在三星上周达成里程碑式的工会协议后变得更为激烈。三星以同意未来10年内将其半导体部门营业利润的10.5%作为股票奖金、另加1.5%的现金奖金，从而避免了为期18天的工厂停工。按照三星近期营业利润估算，这一协议意味着2026年每位芯片部门员工的平均奖金约为34万美元。SK海力士去年9月也达成了类似结构，将营业利润的10%用于员工奖金。对比之下，台积电员工无疑感到痛苦——自1987年成立以来，台积电从未建立工会，也没有正式的集体谈判机制。

员工的沮丧情绪已蔓延至台湾职场社区Dcard以及台积电专属Facebook页面。工人们在公司定于5月28日在新竹总部召开股东大会前纷纷发帖抱怨。有人询问成立工会是否违反台湾法律，也有人指出公司优先考虑股东回报和海外扩张，而非员工福祉。

硅晶圆制造商环球晶圆的董事长徐秀兰对此发表了更广泛的看法。她表示，环球晶圆在9个国家拥有18座工厂，其中有的有工会，有的没有，而商业绩效的关键在于公司是否与员工分享利润，而非是否存在工会。

与此同时，三星的协议已面临法律挑战。一起股东诉讼质疑该协议，理由是承诺将固定比例的营业利润用于员工薪酬长达十年，这与大规模芯片制造对资本的需求相矛盾。

history.titleZh: 面向异构SoC的代理驱动端到端软硬件协同设计基准（哥伦比亚大学，IBM）
history.descriptionZh: 来自哥伦比亚大学和IBM Research的研究团队联合发布了“HSCO-Bench：一个由智能体驱动的端到端软硬件协同设计基准，用于片上系统”。该工作旨在解决当前大型语言模型（LLM）在软件和硬件设计领域评估割裂的问题：现有软件基准通常假设硬件目标固定不变，而硬件基准则聚焦于组件级优化，忽略了完整的系统级协同。HSCO-Bench的核心理念是构建一个端到端的、基于智能体（Agent）的协同设计评估框架，将软件与硬件设计视为一个有机整体。其技术创新在于：利用LLM驱动的智能体，在异构片上系统（SoC）的设计空间中自动探索软硬件划分、接口定义与性能权衡，从而实现对系统级优化能力的统一度量。该基准不仅涵盖CPU、GPU、加速器等异构组件，还引入了动态任务调度与资源管理场景，使得评估结果更能反映真实应用中的协同效率。尽管论文未公开具体数值对比，但作者强调，HSCO-Bench的提出将推动LLM从“单领域代码生成”向“系统全局决策”演进，尤其适用于AI加速器、边缘计算SoC及通信基带芯片等对软硬件协同依赖性极高的场景。该工作的发布有望为学术界和工业界提供一个标准化平台，用于比较不同LLM在复杂系统设计任务中的端到端性能，并启发未来基于大模型的自动化软硬件联合优化工具链。
